records.ety: [L. putativus, fr. putare, putatum, to reckon, suppose, adjust, prune, cleanse. See Pure, and cf. Amputate, Compute, Dispute, Impute.]

defs: Commonly thought or deemed; supposed; reputed; as, the putative father of a child.
quotes: Thus things indifferent, being esteemed useful or pious, became customary, and then came for reverence into a putative and usurped authority.
